name: JCV_NCCR_PML_rearrangement
description: JC virus PML-type NCCR rearrangement: in PML brain tissue, the JCV NCCR exists as two tandemly-duplicated 98-bp blocks (Mad-1 prototype) with deletion of the archetype-specific sequences. The duplication enhances JCV early-gene expression and is required for productive infection of oligodendrocytes. Archetype JCV in urine carries a single 98-bp block plus the deleted-in-PML sequences and is poorly replicative in glia. Detection of rearranged NCCR in CSF or brain biopsy is highly specific for PML.
expected_copy_count: 1 NCCR per genome in either archetype or PML-type configuration
